etymology.description: 乌(烏) (wū, "crow; black") + 鲁(魯) (lǔ, "crass; dull").
etymology.interpretation: The word 乌鲁(烏魯) (wūlǔ) is a transliteration used for the proper noun 'Uru', with no inherent semantic connection between the parts and the meaning. It combines 乌(烏) (wū, "crow; black") which often invokes darkness or blackness due to its literal meaning, and 鲁(魯) (lǔ, "crass; dull"), a character commonly used in transliterations, without a semantic link to the translated entity in this case.
